title: Registered children's nurses
description: Assess patient health problems and needs, develop and implement nursing care plans, and maintain medical records. Administer nursing care to ill, injured, convalescent, or disabled patients. May advise patients on health maintenance and disease prevention or provide case management. Licensing or registration required. Provide advanced nursing care for patients with acute conditions such as heart attacks, respiratory distress syndrome, or shock. May care for pre- and post-operative patients or perform advanced, invasive diagnostic or therapeutic procedures. Assess, diagnose, and treat individuals and families with mental health or substance use disorders or the potential for such disorders. Apply therapeutic activities, including the prescription of medication, per state regulations, and the administration of psychotherapy. (Based on 7 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Maintain accurate, detailed reports and records.
Evaluate and monitor patients' responses to post-surgical care and take corrective actions as needed.
Identify and ensure proper storage and functionality of medical equipment or devices.
Assess patients' emotional and psychosocial needs and provide support or intervention as necessary.
Coordinate and participate in patient care conferences, including the development and evaluation of care plans and protocols.
Administer medications and monitor patients for adverse reactions, side effects, and response to treatment.
Assist in diagnostic procedures and manage patients' airway or pulmonary status during care.
Collaborate with healthcare professionals and interdisciplinary teams to create or adjust patient treatment plans based on assessments.
Monitor, document, and report symptoms or changes in patients' conditions, including vital signs and laboratory data.
Educate patients and their families about health maintenance, medications, and treatment plans.
Assess urgent and emergent health conditions using physiologically and technologically derived data.
Assess the impact of illnesses or injuries on patients' health, function, development, nutrition, and quality of life.
Document data related to patients' care, including assessment results, interventions, medications, and patient responses.
Discuss illnesses and treatments with patients and family members to ensure understanding and informed consent.
Diagnose psychiatric disorders and mental health conditions, and treat accordingly.
Set up, operate, or monitor invasive equipment such as catheters and mechanical ventilators in patient care.
Obtain specimens or samples for laboratory work and interpret diagnostic test results based on assessments.
Administer blood transfusions or intravenous infusions, monitoring patients for adverse reactions during procedures.
Prepare reports documenting patients' care activities and discharge planning.
Coordinate or conduct educational programs on nursing practices and health care topics for staff and patients.
Observe, interview, and assess patients to identify care needs, developing and implementing nursing care plans.
Collaborate with other health care professionals to ensure optimal patient care and continuity of services.
Evaluate the quality and effectiveness of nursing practice and make clinical recommendations for improvements.
Provide specialized direct and indirect care to inpatients and outpatients within designated specialties, like pediatrics.
Develop and implement standards of nursing practice in specialty areas such as pediatrics or acute care.
Direct or supervise nursing care staff in patient therapy and identify training needs for continuous improvement.
Participate in clinical research projects related to nursing practice and patient care methods.
Conduct individual or group psychotherapy for patients with mental health disorders, assessing treatment efficacy.
Prescribe medications and observe patients' reactions, modifying prescriptions as necessary based on patient needs.
Refer patients to specialty consultations or treatments based on assessed health needs and care plans.
